pattern: An in-order walk of a search tree already visits values from smallest to largest, so recording that visitation order and re-linking it as a pure right-leaning chain reproduces the rearrangement directly.
hints: An in-order traversal of a binary search tree yields its values in ascending order; that traversal order is exactly the shape of the final answer.
Build a new chain where each node's left child is always empty and its right child is the next value in that ascending order.
A dummy head node makes it easy to attach the first real value and then keep extending the chain to the right as you go.

edge_inputs.description: already a right-leaning chain, output should be unchanged in shape
a fully left-leaning chain must be completely reversed into a right-leaning one
single node at the maximum value bound

stress.description: random binary search tree from a shuffled permutation of unique values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100)))
    vals = rng.sample(range(0, 1001), n)
    order = vals[:]
    rng.shuffle(order)
    root = None
    nodes = {}
    def insert(v):
        nonlocal root
        if root is None:
            root = v
            nodes[v] = [None, None]
            return
        cur = root
        while True:
            l, r = nodes[cur]
            if v < cur:
                if l is None:
                    nodes[cur][0] = v
                    nodes[v] = [None, None]
                    return
                cur = l
            else:
                if r is None:
                    nodes[cur][1] = v
                    nodes[v] = [None, None]
                    return
                cur = r
    for v in order:
        insert(v)
    from collections import deque
    out = []
    q = deque([root])
    while q:
        cur = q.popleft()
        if cur is None:
            out.append(None)
            continue
        out.append(cur)
        cl, cr = nodes[cur]
        q.append(cl)
        q.append(cr)
    while out and out[-1] is None:
        out.pop()
    return [out]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def increasingBST(self, root):
        dummy = TreeNode(0)
        self._cur = dummy
        def relink(node):
            if not node:
                return
            relink(node.left)
            node.left = None
            self._cur.right = node
            self._cur = node
            relink(node.right)
        relink(root)
        return dummy.right
